fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

prompt:
You are working on the TaskFlow Pro project at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-eNeZ0F.

Your task: Design and implement an analytics dashboard API.

1. Create analytics models in src/models/analytics.ts:
   - AnalyticsSummary (totalUsers, totalOrders, revenue, activeUsers)
   - UserAnalytics (userId, orderCount, totalSpent, lastActive)
   - TrendPoint (timestamp, metric, value)

2. Create an AnalyticsService in src/services/analytics.service.ts:
   - getSummary() — aggregate stats from UserRepository and OrderRepository
   - getUserAnalytics(userId) — per-user analytics
   - getTrends(metric, startDate, endDate) — time-series data

3. Add tests for the analytics service

Follow the existing repository and service patterns. Use dependency injection via constructor parameters.

parameters.prompt:
Explore the TaskFlow Pro project at /private/var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-eNeZ0F. I need to understand:

1. The directory structure (src/models, src/services, src/repositories, tests)
2. Existing model patterns (read a few model files to see interfaces/types used)
3. Existing service patterns (read a service file to see dependency injection, constructor patterns)
4. Existing repository patterns (especially UserRepository and OrderRepository - what methods they expose)
5. Existing test patterns (how tests are structured, what testing framework is used)

Thoroughness: very thorough. Read the actual file contents, not just list files. Report back the full contents of key files.

parameters.task: Design and implement analytics dashboard API: models, service, and tests

alternatives.option: Add an AnalyticsRepository for caching aggregated data
alternatives.reason_rejected: Over-engineering for current in-memory DB; repositories are for entity persistence, not computed aggregates

parameters.context: Need analytics dashboard API with summary, per-user analytics, and time-series trends
parameters.rationale: Follows existing service patterns: constructor-based DI, Logger instance, repository dependencies. No EventBus needed since analytics is read-only. Trends group by day using ISO date keys for natural bucketing.
parameters.summary: Analytics service aggregates from UserRepository and OrderRepository with constructor DI

parameters.detail: Created 3 files: src/models/analytics.ts (AnalyticsSummary, UserAnalytics, TrendPoint interfaces), src/services/analytics.service.ts (AnalyticsService with getSummary, getUserAnalytics, getTrends), tests/services/analytics.service.test.ts (13 tests, all passing). Follows existing DI and repository patterns.
parameters.summary: Analytics dashboard API implemented: models, service, and 13 passing tests
